rusty_pipe.Name: Rusty Pipe
rusty_pipe.Desc: A length of corroded pipe. The tetanus is free.
rusty_pipe.Lore: Found near every entrance to the dungeon. Previous crawlers left them behind when they found something better. Or when they stopped needing weapons.

pipe_wrench.Name: Pipe Wrench
pipe_wrench.Lore: Standard-issue dungeon starter kit item. GalaCorps markets these as 'Crawler Survival Tools.' They are pipe wrenches.

kitchen_knife.Name: Kitchen Knife
kitchen_knife.Desc: From someone's kitchen. Fast, sharp, and deeply domestic.
kitchen_knife.Lore: A previous crawler's note was found with one of these: 'It's a chef's knife. I am not a chef. I am making do.'

boxcutter.Name: Boxcutter
boxcutter.Lore: GalaCorps workplace safety disclaimer: 'Boxcutters are not approved for use against sentient beings.' GalaCorps also turned Earth into a dungeon, so.

combat_knife.Name: Combat Knife
combat_knife.Desc: Serrated edge, polymer grip. Designed for this.
combat_knife.Lore: Pre-dungeon military surplus. Whoever stocked these down here was either prescient or the entire apocalypse was a supply chain optimization.

spiked_bat.Name: Spiked Bat
spiked_bat.Desc: A bat with nails hammered through it. Crude. Works.
spiked_bat.Lore: Crafted by a crawler known as Nails McGee. Nails McGee is dead. The bat lives on.

shortsword.Name: Shortsword
shortsword.Desc: Classic, balanced, reliable. Not exciting. Still dangerous.
shortsword.Lore: The Dungeon Armorers Guild (if it existed) would rate this three stars out of five. Efficient. Respectable.

machete.Name: Machete
machete.Desc: Long, heavy, unsubtle. Excellent in jungle biomes.
machete.Lore: Manufactured by TropoCorp Survival Systems. The brochure says 'for clearing vegetation.' The dungeon has other vegetation.

goblin_cleaver.Name: Goblin Cleaver
goblin_cleaver.Desc: Thick, jagged, and smells faintly of explosives.
goblin_cleaver.Lore: Looted off the most dangerous goblin you've met so far. This is not the most dangerous goblin you will meet.

blessed_dagger.Name: Blessed Dagger
blessed_dagger.Desc: Warm to the touch. Slightly angry about undead.
blessed_dagger.Lore: Blessed by a priest who didn't survive the first floor. His blessing did. Deal +5 bonus damage against undead enemies.

magic_staff.Name: Magic Staff
magic_staff.Desc: Channels dungeon energy into offensive blasts. Smells like ozone.
magic_staff.Lore: Product description from DungeonMart: 'The Arcanist's Companion, v2.3. Now with 12% more arcane throughput.' Scales with Intelligence.

enchanted_bat.Name: Enchanted Bat
enchanted_bat.Desc: The enchantment makes a sound like a home run every time it connects.
enchanted_bat.Lore: Was mundane until a mage dropped it. The mage was not the last thing it hit.

thunderhammer.Name: Thunderhammer
thunderhammer.Desc: Hits everything in range. The thunder is not metaphorical.
thunderhammer.Lore: GalaCorps Area-Effect Percussion Tool, Model T-7. Designed for 'crowd management.' Hits all enemies in combat for reduced damage.

plasma_cutter.Name: Plasma Cutter
plasma_cutter.Desc: Industrial mining tool repurposed into something a lot less safe.
plasma_cutter.Lore: Mining Union Local 7 issued these before the dungeon integration. The union is gone. The cutters remain.

chainsaw.Name: Chainsaw
chainsaw.Desc: Extremely loud. Extremely effective. Extremely telegraphed.
chainsaw.Lore: Note from previous crawler: 'Great damage, but the noise attracted three goblin patrols. Worth it. Mostly.' The noise increases your encounter rate by 20% while equipped.

rune_blade.Name: Rune Blade
rune_blade.Desc: Alien runes carved into the blade. They glow when near enemies. Everything is near enemies.
rune_blade.Lore: Manufactured in the seventh sector by Vreth Armaments Ltd. 'Guaranteed to slay one (1) dungeon boss. Warranty void if boss is sentient.' They're all sentient.

void_blade.Name: Void Blade
void_blade.Desc: Cuts through armor like it isn't there. Occasionally cuts through things that aren't there.
void_blade.Lore: Forged in the void between floors by something that doesn't have a name. Previous owner: unknown. Whereabouts of previous owner: also unknown.

bossbane.Name: Bossbane
bossbane.Desc: It knows when you're fighting a boss. It gets excited.
bossbane.Lore: Commissioned by a crawler consortium after the fourth floor. They pooled resources to make one weapon specifically designed for killing the things that keep killing crawlers.

jugs_o_boom.Name: Jug O' Boom
jugs_o_boom.Desc: A large ceramic jug filled with something that should not exist in ceramic.
jugs_o_boom.Lore: GalaCorps Product Recall Notice #4471: 'If you have encountered a Jug O' Boom in the dungeon, do not attempt to examine, open, or throw it.' They really shouldn't have put that last part.

torn_jeans.Name: Torn Jeans
torn_jeans.Desc: Denim that's seen better days. Better than nothing. Barely.
torn_jeans.Lore: You were probably wearing these when you arrived. Or found them in a pile. Dungeon doesn't care.

work_shirt.Name: Work Shirt
work_shirt.Desc: Heavy canvas work shirt. One previous owner. Previous owner is not available for comment.
work_shirt.Lore: From the pre-dungeon era when 'work' meant something other than survival. The name patch says 'Dave.'

leather_jacket.Name: Leather Jacket
leather_jacket.Desc: Surprisingly protective. The studs are decorative but make you look serious.
leather_jacket.Lore: Popular with second-floor survivors. The GalaCorps viewer ratings go up when crawlers wear these. Nobody knows why.

kevlar_vest.Name: Kevlar Vest
kevlar_vest.Desc: Rated to stop small arms fire. Small arms fire is not the primary concern in here.
kevlar_vest.Lore: Pre-dungeon police surplus. Still rated for what it was rated for. The dungeon has significantly upgraded its offerings since then.

riot_gear.Name: Riot Gear
riot_gear.Desc: Full body protection designed for civil unrest. Civil unrest was the warmup.
riot_gear.Lore: Found in the police precinct on floor three. The cops who wore it didn't need it anymore. Different reason than you'd hope.

hazmat_suit.Name: Hazmat Suit
hazmat_suit.Desc: Full environmental protection. Heavy. Effective. Grants immunity to poison.
hazmat_suit.Lore: GalaCorps Biohazard Response Suit, Mark IV. Designed for chemical spills. Repurposed for dungeons with plague rats, corpse bloats, and whatever floor two is doing to the water supply.

dungeon_plate.Name: Dungeon Plate
dungeon_plate.Desc: Salvaged and reforged dungeon-grade metal. Heavy, loud, effective.
dungeon_plate.Lore: Made by a blacksmith who refused to stop being a blacksmith even when everything ended. Sold to crawlers. She is still down here somewhere, and she is doing fine.

battle_plate.Name: Battle Plate
battle_plate.Desc: Enchanted plate mail. Glows faintly. Enemies notice.
battle_plate.Lore: Awarded to the top 0.01% of surviving crawlers in the fourth annual GalaCorps Dungeon Survivor Sweepstakes. The sweepstakes had thirty contestants. One survived.

celestial_weave.Name: Celestial Weave
celestial_weave.Desc: Light as cloth, harder than steel, and it never needs washing. Important.
celestial_weave.Lore: Woven from materials that don't exist in this dimension. GalaCorps catalog entry: 'The Celestial Weave: because even crawlers deserve to die looking good.'

void_suit.Name: Void Suit
void_suit.Desc: Armor designed for the void between floors. You're not in the void. It still works.
void_suit.Lore: Recovered from the ninth floor by exactly two people. One of them is you, right now, reading this description.

crawler_exo.Name: Crawler Exo
crawler_exo.Desc: Full exoskeleton chassis. You look like a boss encounter.
crawler_exo.Lore: GalaCorps Endgame Crawler Merchandise Collection. 'Reach floor ten looking like you belong there.' Viewer subscriptions increase by 1000 when equipped.

iron_knuckles.Name: Iron Knuckles
iron_knuckles.Desc: Brass would have been classier but iron was available.
iron_knuckles.Lore: Found in a gym locker on floor two. The gym membership is still valid, technically. The gym is now a swamp.

crawler_tag.Name: Crawler Tag
crawler_tag.Desc: GalaCorps official viewer tracking tag. Slightly invasive. Significantly profitable.
crawler_tag.Lore: GalaCorps Crawler Registration tag. By wearing this, you agree to Section 47B of the Galactic Entertainment and Survival Agreement, which you did not read. Grants +500 viewers.

speed_bracer.Name: Speed Bracer
speed_bracer.Desc: Tightens reaction time through mild electrokinetic stimulation. The mild part is debatable.
speed_bracer.Lore: Pre-dungeon sports performance tech. GalaCorps repackaged them as 'Crawler Initiative Enhancement Tools' and tripled the price.

blessed_ring.Name: Blessed Ring
blessed_ring.Desc: Something good lingers in the metal. Hard to say what. Seems helpful.
blessed_ring.Lore: Passed down through four crawlers before reaching you. The previous owners blessed it themselves, which is not how blessings work, but the dungeon doesn't care.

battle_focus.Name: Battle Focus
battle_focus.Desc: A headband with a stabilizing rune. Keeps your hands steady and your mind clear.
battle_focus.Lore: DungeonMart Athletic Division: 'Zone in, tune out, hit harder.' Approved for competitive dungeon crawling by the GalaCorps Crawler Association.

mana_ring.Name: Mana Ring
mana_ring.Desc: A smooth silver ring that hums faintly. Expand your capacity. Slightly.
mana_ring.Lore: Manufactured by ArcaneWorks Galactic. 'For the discerning dungeon mage. Maximum mana +3. Minimum dignity preserved.'

dungeon_compass.Name: Dungeon Compass
dungeon_compass.Desc: Points toward exits instead of north. An extremely pragmatic design choice.
dungeon_compass.Lore: Commissioned by a survivor collective on floor two. 'We kept walking in circles,' they wrote. 'Now we only walk in spirals.'

tactical_knife.Name: Tactical Knife
tactical_knife.Desc: Worn on the forearm. Deployable in 0.3 seconds. Does not count as a weapon slot.
tactical_knife.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Research found that crawlers with backup blades generated 18% more 'imminent peril' ratings. Production ramped up accordingly.

carbon_fiber_vest.Name: Carbon Fiber Vest
carbon_fiber_vest.Desc: Lightweight armor insert. Doesn't slow you down. Protects you a bit. Looks professional.
carbon_fiber_vest.Lore: Military contractor issue. Found in a supply cache on floor six labeled 'For Authorized Personnel Only.' Nobody authorized is left.

enchanted_bracer.Name: Enchanted Bracer
enchanted_bracer.Desc: Defense rune on one face, amplification rune on the other. Multitasking.
enchanted_bracer.Lore: Made by the same gnome enchanter who made the enchanted bat. He has opinions about accessories. They're not wrong.

berserker_band.Name: Berserker Band
berserker_band.Desc: Pumps combat stimulants into your bloodstream. Side effects include victory and regret.
berserker_band.Lore: GalaCorps Medical Division: 'Not approved for human use. Not approved for most alien use. Phenomenal viewer ratings.' ATK +4, DEF -2 while equipped.

bandage.Name: Bandage
bandage.Desc: A roll of gauze. Works on small wounds. Medium wounds. Maybe.
bandage.Lore: GalaCorps Basic Medical Supply. 'Restores 12 HP. Not responsible for any wounds that exceed the rated capacity of this bandage.'

field_ration.Name: Field Ration
field_ration.Desc: A sealed foil packet of something technically nutritious.
field_ration.Lore: Military ration, sealed 3 years before the dungeon formed. Flavor: Beige. Nutrition: Adequate. Morale effect: Negative.

antidote.Name: Antidote
antidote.Lore: GalaCorps Toxin Neutralizer v2. Warning: 'Not effective against void toxins, psychic venom, or existential dread.' Cures standard poison debuff.

mana_crystal_small.Name: Small Mana Crystal
mana_crystal_small.Desc: A faintly glowing shard. Restores a small amount of mana.
mana_crystal_small.Lore: Naturally forming dungeon crystal. GalaCorps collects them in bulk, repackages them, marks them up 400%. The dungeon economy is functioning as intended.

dungeon_bread.Name: Dungeon Bread
dungeon_bread.Desc: Coarse, dense, and aggressively sustaining. It wasn't made lovingly but it was made.
dungeon_bread.Lore: Baked by a ghost on floor three who cannot stop baking. She doesn't know why she keeps baking. The bread is good though.

energy_drink.Name: Energy Drink
energy_drink.Desc: Contains something called 'Dungeon Taurine.' Don't look that up.
energy_drink.Lore: GalaCorps Endurance Beverage: CRAWLER FUEL. 'For 3-10 floor performance.' Side effects may include temporary invincibility delusion.

health_potion.Name: Health Potion
health_potion.Desc: Red, thick, and heals things. Don't drink two.
health_potion.Lore: GalaCorps Vitality Supplement Series 1. 'Restores up to 30 HP. May cause brief involuntary recollections of better times.'

holy_water.Name: Holy Water
holy_water.Desc: Blessed by someone who meant it. Effective against undead. Tastes fine.
holy_water.Lore: Bottled by a priest crawler who has made it his mission. 'While I live, they won't,' he says about undead. He has survived seven floors. Heals 15 HP normally, 40 HP vs undead.

adrenal_shot.Name: Adrenal Shot
adrenal_shot.Desc: An injection that makes you temporarily terrifying. Temporarily.
adrenal_shot.Lore: GalaCorps Combat Chemistry Dept. 'Adrenal Augmentation Compound, 2-Round Formula. Not for use by anyone with a functioning sense of self-preservation.' +4 ATK for 2 rounds.

speed_shot.Name: Speed Shot
speed_shot.Desc: Everything slows down. You didn't slow down.
speed_shot.Lore: GalaCorps Combat Chemistry Dept. 'Velocity Augmentation Compound, 2-Round Formula. Warning: walls are faster than you think.' +3 SPD for 2 rounds.

berserker_serum.Name: Berserker Serum
berserker_serum.Desc: Maximum offense, minimum sense. Not endorsed by any medical body.
berserker_serum.Lore: GalaCorps Combat Chemistry Dept. 'Berserker Protocol Serum.' Legal found this one. They had a lot of notes. +6 ATK, -3 DEF for 2 rounds.

stim_pack.Name: Stim Pack
stim_pack.Desc: Military-grade recovery pack. Jabs itself in. You will feel this later.
stim_pack.Lore: Pre-dungeon military medical. Administered automatically if you're incapacitated. The auto-administration mechanism is 'a big needle.'

darkness_potion.Name: Darkness Potion
darkness_potion.Desc: Makes you harder to hit for a brief window. Side effect: everything looks purple.
darkness_potion.Lore: From the alchemist on floor four who went by 'Madame Obsidian.' She's gone now. Her potions are still very good. +4 DEF for 3 rounds.

goblin_grenade.Name: Goblin Grenade
goblin_grenade.Desc: Looted from a goblin sapper. Don't hold it too long.
goblin_grenade.Lore: Goblin Ordnance Solutions, Div. of GobCorp. 'Our grenades are certified to explode within ±5 seconds of desired detonation time.' Throwable: 25 damage.

flash_bomb.Name: Flash Bomb
flash_bomb.Desc: Produces a blinding flash. Stuns enemy for 1 round.
flash_bomb.Lore: Security surplus from the pre-dungeon GalaCorps monitoring stations on what was then the surface. Effective then. Effective now.

mana_vial.Name: Mana Vial
mana_vial.Desc: A sealed glass vial of concentrated dungeon mana. Restores 3 MP.
mana_vial.Lore: DungeonMart Arcane Division: 'Mana Replenishment Solution, 3MP Grade.' Fine print: 'Not responsible for mana overflow events.'

antiparasitic.Name: Antiparasitic
antiparasitic.Desc: Clears parasitic infestations and recovers a bit of HP. Effective against grubs, leeches, and that one thing.
antiparasitic.Lore: GalaCorps Medical: 'Floor two survivors report a 90% rate of parasitic contact. Antiparasitic availability is currently 12%. We are aware of this discrepancy.'

sanity_tonic.Name: Sanity Tonic
sanity_tonic.Desc: Clears psychic debuffs and restores mana. Tastes like grape.
sanity_tonic.Lore: DungeonMart Mental Health Division: 'Sanity Tonic: for when the dungeon gets in your head. Literally.' Restores 5 MP and removes fear/madness effects.

mega_health.Name: Mega Health Potion
mega_health.Desc: The big one. It is not subtle.
mega_health.Lore: GalaCorps Vitality Supplement Series MEGA. 'For crawlers who refuse to die yet.' Endorsed by seven surviving crawlers, all of whom are now dead for other reasons.

greater_mana.Name: Greater Mana Potion
greater_mana.Desc: A full mana restoration solution. For the serious spellcaster.
greater_mana.Lore: ArcaneWorks Galactic: 'Greater Mana Solution, 6MP Grade.' Product warning: 'Consuming more than two in one floor has been associated with minor reality instability.'

scroll_of_fire.Name: Scroll of Fire
scroll_of_fire.Desc: A single-use spell scroll. Casts Fireball without MP cost.
scroll_of_fire.Lore: Pre-inscribed by a mage crawler who mass-produced these on floor four before the conductor lich expressed its displeasure. The scrolls outlasted the mage.

scroll_of_ice.Name: Scroll of Ice
scroll_of_ice.Desc: A single-use scroll that casts Frost Shard. Slows target for 1 round.
scroll_of_ice.Lore: Same mage, same floor four operation. Cold is underrated in this dungeon. Also useful for preserving food, but that's not why you're here.

tome_of_power.Name: Tome of Power
tome_of_power.Desc: Teaches the Static Bolt spell permanently on use. One time only.
tome_of_power.Lore: Written in the first week of the dungeon by someone who clearly knew what was happening before everyone else did. 'You will need this,' it says on the cover. They weren't wrong.

sponsors_box.Name: Sponsor's Box
sponsors_box.Desc: A silver-tier loot box from your sponsors. They're watching.
sponsors_box.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Engagement Package: Silver Tier. 'Your sponsors appreciate your continued survival and look forward to your eventual death.'

iron_loot_box.Name: Iron Loot Box
iron_loot_box.Desc: The entry-level loot box. What you get when you do the minimum to survive.
iron_loot_box.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Engagement Package: Iron Tier. 'Thanks for not dying yet. Here is a box.'

bronze_box.Name: Bronze Box
bronze_box.Desc: Bronze-tier viewer loot box. Slightly better than iron.
bronze_box.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Engagement Package: Bronze Tier. 'Your kill count has earned you slightly more consideration. Slightly.'

gold_box.Name: Gold Box
gold_box.Desc: Gold-tier viewer loot box. Your sponsors are impressed.
gold_box.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Engagement Package: Gold Tier. 'Congratulations on reaching a floor that most of our actuarial models said you wouldn't.'

celestial_box.Name: Celestial Box
celestial_box.Desc: The highest-tier loot box. It glows. Viewers are rioting in excitement.
celestial_box.Lore: GalaCorps Viewer Engagement Package: Celestial Tier. 'Reserved for crawlers who have achieved the statistically impossible. We see you. So does everyone else.'

transit_card.Name: Transit Card
transit_card.Desc: An old subway card. Has 2 rides left. The train is still running.
transit_card.Lore: Loaded card from the old transit system. The dungeon integrated the trains but kept the fare gates. Somewhere, a municipal accountant is very proud.

castle_banner_1.Name: Castle Banner: Eagle
castle_banner_1.Desc: One of four banners needed to open the castle gate on floor five.
castle_banner_1.Lore: The castle was built by someone who had very specific opinions about heraldry. The banners must be collected in order. The order matters. Ask a ghost.

castle_banner_2.Name: Castle Banner: Serpent
castle_banner_2.Desc: Second of four banners. Serpent sigil on faded blue.
castle_banner_2.Lore: The castle's original owner favored serpents. This is consistent with everything else you've found down here.

castle_banner_3.Name: Castle Banner: Skull
castle_banner_3.Desc: Third of four banners. The skull has a little crown on it.
castle_banner_3.Lore: The castle's heraldic committee was clearly trying to say something. You're not sure what. You're holding a banner with a crowned skull in a dungeon.

castle_banner_4.Name: Castle Banner: Crown
castle_banner_4.Desc: Fourth and final banner. Just a crown. Understated, somehow.
castle_banner_4.Lore: The final banner is the plainest. 'All roads lead here,' notes a previous crawler on the back. The gate opens when all four are presented. The castle does not.

monster_card.Name: Monster Card
monster_card.Desc: A GalaCorps collector card. Depicts a dungeon monster in unsettling detail.
monster_card.Lore: GalaCorps Dungeon Monster Collector Series. 'Collect all 200! Trade with other crawlers!' Trading requires encountering other crawlers, which is statistically unlikely.

hunters_trophy.Name: Hunter's Trophy
hunters_trophy.Desc: Proof of kill. A galactic hunter's license tag, presented to their prey. Ironic.
hunters_trophy.Lore: Taken from Vrah'keth after the fight. It says 'Verified Kill' in seventeen languages. You are the first entity listed as the verified killer rather than the kill.

core_fragment.Name: Core Fragment
core_fragment.Desc: A shard of the dungeon AI core. It hums. It watches you.
core_fragment.Lore: GalaCorps Priority Recovery Object: Code RED. 'If found, do not touch. Do not carry. Do not use in any ritual context.' You found it. You touched it. You're carrying it.

scrap_metal.Name: Scrap Metal
scrap_metal.Desc: Bent, corroded, and useful for making things.
scrap_metal.Lore: The dungeon runs on old infrastructure. The infrastructure runs on this.

goblin_powder.Name: Goblin Powder
goblin_powder.Desc: Explosive compound recovered from goblin remains. Handle carefully. Very carefully.
goblin_powder.Lore: Drops from exploding goblins and goblin sappers. The goblins were carrying it for a reason. Now you are carrying it. Consider why.

monster_hide.Name: Monster Hide
monster_hide.Desc: Thick, durable, and smells like whatever you killed.
monster_hide.Lore: Harvested from beast enemies. Used in light armor crafting and accessory modifications. DungeonMart buyback rate: 6 gold. DungeonMart resale rate: 40 gold.

iron_shard.Name: Iron Shard
iron_shard.Desc: A fragment of iron. More useful than it looks.
iron_shard.Lore: Broken off from constructs and iron golems. The golems were not happy about this.

chemical_jug.Name: Chemical Jug
chemical_jug.Lore: Contents unknown. GalaCorps Material Safety Department filed seventeen reports about these. None were actioned. Used in explosive and poison item crafting.

copper_wire.Name: Copper Wire
copper_wire.Desc: Coiled wire salvaged from dungeon infrastructure. Used in construct-related crafts.
copper_wire.Lore: The dungeon runs on power. The power runs on wire. The wire comes from somewhere nobody's been yet.

explosive_gel.Name: Explosive Gel
explosive_gel.Desc: Stable until it isn't. Used in traps and thrown weapons.
explosive_gel.Lore: GalaCorps Demolitions Division: 'Explosive Gel, Formula 7. Cleared for dungeon use by Section 12 of the Crawler Combat Authorization Act.' There is no Section 12.

binding_gel.Name: Binding Gel
binding_gel.Desc: Adheres things to other things. Permanently. Don't ask how you'd un-adhere them.
binding_gel.Lore: Multi-use dungeon adhesive. Useful for reinforcing armor, attaching components, and the occasional tactical application. Do not get on your hands.

dungeon_crystal.Name: Dungeon Crystal
dungeon_crystal.Desc: A naturally forming dungeon energy crystal. Used in enchantments.
dungeon_crystal.Lore: Forms naturally at floor transition points. GalaCorps harvests them in bulk. The leftovers fall to lower floors where crawlers pick them up. GalaCorps is aware.

dungeon_ore.Name: Dungeon Ore
dungeon_ore.Desc: Dense, magically conductive ore. The backbone of dungeon-grade weapons.
dungeon_ore.Lore: Mined from floor veins by gnome engineers before they decided they'd rather fight crawlers. Their loss.

arcane_dust.Name: Arcane Dust
arcane_dust.Desc: Powdered dungeon-grade enchantment material. Glows faintly. Don't breathe it.
arcane_dust.Lore: ArcaneWorks Galactic Catalog Entry: 'Arcane Dust, Grade 3. Used in weapon enchantment, armor runing, and a variety of mildly hazardous applications.'

void_essence.Name: Void Essence
void_essence.Desc: A vial of distilled void energy. Available floor 6+. Handle with gloves.
void_essence.Lore: Extracted from void-type enemies by crawlers with specific crafting skills. Required for top-tier item construction. GalaCorps has a buy-back program for these. The rate is insulting.

duct_tape.Name: Duct Tape
duct_tape.Lore: Pre-dungeon era hardware staple. The dungeon has managed to make everything worse except duct tape, which remains the most universally useful object in existence.

donut_biscuit.Name: Donut Biscuit
donut_biscuit.Lore: Dungeon-generated food item, category: comfort. GalaCorps taste-tested these before approving them for dungeon distribution. The test panel gave them 2/10. They were approved anyway.

mordecai_scroll.Name: Mordecai's Scroll
mordecai_scroll.Desc: A densely written scroll from a crawler named Mordecai. Contains useful dungeon intelligence.
mordecai_scroll.Lore: Left by Mordecai, who made it to floor nine before stopping to write down everything he knew. He did not make it to floor ten. His notes did.

lockpick.Name: Lockpick
lockpick.Desc: Thin, flexible, and the difference between stuck and not stuck.
lockpick.Lore: A pre-dungeon skill that suddenly became extremely relevant. Whoever stocked these in dungeon supply caches was thinking ahead, or was a criminal, or both.

smoke_bomb.Name: Smoke Bomb
smoke_bomb.Desc: Fills the corridor with smoke. Useful for leaving. Not useful for staying.
smoke_bomb.Lore: Originally developed for theatrical use. The theatre is gone. The smoke bombs remain. Allows escape from combat with 85% success rate.

map_neighborhood.Name: Neighborhood Map
map_neighborhood.Desc: Reveals the full layout of the current neighborhood zone.
map_neighborhood.Lore: Printed by the GalaCorps Crawler Orientation Department. 'You are here. Everything else is trying to kill you.'

map_borough.Name: Borough Map
map_borough.Desc: Reveals the full layout of the current borough.
map_borough.Lore: GalaCorps Cartographic Division: 'Borough Map, Edition 3. Updated to reflect recent structural modifications.' The modifications are hostile.

map_city.Name: City Map
map_city.Desc: Full city-level dungeon map. Exits, bosses, shops — all marked.
map_city.Lore: GalaCorps Cartographic Division: 'City Map, classified.' How you got it is your business. The information inside is worth the risk.

map_province.Name: Province Map
map_province.Desc: Province-level dungeon map. Shows floors 7-9 in full.
map_province.Lore: GalaCorps Executive Access Map. Reserved for dungeon administrators and, apparently, whoever found this one.

map_country.Name: Country Map
map_country.Desc: Full dungeon map, all ten floors. Everything marked. Everything.
map_country.Lore: GalaCorps Master Dungeon Schematics. 'Authorized personnel only.' There are no authorized personnel left. Use this wisely.
